feature.name: openedr-style-apc-injection
feature.title: Kernel DLL injection: migrate to OpenEDR LoadLibraryExW APC (no shellcode)
feature.description:
OpenEDR queues a user-mode APC with: NormalRoutine = kernel32!LoadLibraryExW NormalContext = LPWSTR dll path in target (PAGE_READWRITE only) SystemArgument1 = NULL (hFile) SystemArgument2 = LOAD_IGNORE_CODE_AUTHZ_LEVEL | LOAD_LIBRARY_SEARCH_SYSTEM32 (or equivalent flags; verify on target Windows builds)
Peregrine currently: allocates shellcode + UNICODE_STRING + path protects shellcode RX NormalRoutine = shellcode calling ntdll!LdrLoadDll leaves staging until process exit (VAD self-noise)
Target end state: same OpenEDR APC shape; no executable staging; free path buffer ASAP (process exit minimum; better: after DLL mapped if safe) retain target allowlist + auto StateAddPid + x86/x64 DLL paths

task.title: Port / implement OpenEDR APC queue helpers
task.description:
Adapt OpenEDR apcqueue (KeInitializeApc / KeInsertQueueApc / KernelRoutine frees KAPC, RundownRoutine) into Peregrine style (C, existing pool tags, no C++ required unless we choose to keep C).
Requirements: - UserMode APC, OriginalApcEnvironment - NormalRoutine = resolved LoadLibraryExW address (not shellcode) - NormalContext = pointer to wide path in target process - insert(SystemArgument1=NULL, SystemArgument2=flags) - WoW64: PsWrapApcWow64Thread on context + routine before KeInitializeApc / insert (mirror OpenEDR apcinjector doInject Wow64 branch)
License/credit: header comment pointing at OpenEDR injectengine sources is fine.

task.title: Resolve LoadLibraryExW per target process (kernel32 map)
task.description:
OpenEDR tracks per-PID bases of ntdll/kernel32/user32 on LoadImageNotify, then getProcAddress(kernel32, "LoadLibraryExW").
Minimum viable for Peregrine: - On image load of kernel32.dll (native System32 vs SysWOW64), parse exports of that image base in the attached/current process context and resolve LoadLibraryExW. - Prefer per-PID resolution at inject time (ASLR-safe) over a single global cache, or cache per pending PID once kernel32 is seen.
Can port OpenEDR petools getProcAddress logic, or reuse/extend Peregrine ParseExports currently used for LdrLoadDll.
Trigger remains: kernel32 load + PID on pending injection list (keep AC target model).

task.title: Replace DoInjectInContext: path-only alloc + LoadLibraryExW APC
task.description:
Remove: - g_ShellcodeX64 / g_ShellcodeX86 and patch offsets - UNICODE_STRING build for LdrLoadDll - ZwProtectVirtualMemory to PAGE_EXECUTE_READ for inject stub
New DoInject (or rewrite of DoInjectInContext): 1. Choose x64 vs x86 DLL path from g_Inj (existing) 2. ZwAllocateVirtualMemory(PAGE_READWRITE) sized for path + null only 3. Copy wide path into allocation 4. Resolve LoadLibraryExW (task 2) 5. Queue APC: NormalRoutine=LoadLibraryExW, Context=path, flags in SystemArg2 6. Optional: KeTestAlertThread(UserMode) — keep if still needed for reliable delivery 7. Track allocation for free (task 5) 8. On success: StateAddPid(ProcessId) (keep)
DLL path note: OpenEDR often injects by filename under System32 search flags. Peregrine uses full paths under C:\Peregrine\ — verify LoadLibraryExW flags: - Prefer full path + flags that do not break loading from outside System32 - LOAD_LIBRARY_SEARCH_SYSTEM32 alone may be wrong for C:\Peregrine\*.dll - Use full absolute path; set flags carefully (possibly 0, or LOAD_WITH_ALTERED_SEARCH_PATH / documented safe flags for absolute paths). Document chosen flags in code comments after VM verification.

task.title: Wire notify path; remove dead LdrLoadDll ntdll export dependency for inject
task.description:
InjOnImageLoad: - Keep pending match + kernel32 trigger - Call new inject path instead of shellcode DoInjectInContext - ntdll base caching only needed if still used elsewhere; if only for LdrLoadDll, drop or narrow it
InjOnProcessCreate / InjOnProcessExit: - Keep target matching, pending list, StateRemovePid, free staging
InjOnThreadCreate: - Still stub unless we later port OpenEDR first-thread / retry logic (optional follow-up)
Process filter (optional, recommended): - Skip PP/PPL processes like OpenEDR isAllowDllInjection — if inject would fail or is undesirable.

task.title: Memory free: path buffer only, sooner if safe
task.description:
OpenEDR frees user path memory on process terminate (and has freelist on thread exit).
Peregrine: - Reuse/simplify PendingFree* for path-only allocations (no RX region) - Minimum: free on process exit (InjOnProcessExit) — already have pattern - Better: free after our DLL image maps — only if LoadLibraryExW has finished and no longer needs the path buffer. OpenEDR intentionally delays free; if unsure, free on exit only (still huge win vs RX shellcode page).
Do not free while Ldr/LoadLibrary still holds a pointer to the path.

task.title: IOCTL / config surface stays compatible
task.description:
Keep existing commands: - set x64/x86 DLL path (8/9) - add injection target (10) - enable/disable (11) - clear targets (14)
No userland API break required if paths remain full paths to PeregrineDLL_*.dll. Update any comments in Coms.c / driver_comm.rs that mention shellcode or LdrLoadDll.

task.title: README + code comments: document OpenEDR-style injection
task.description:
Update README "Kernel APC DLL Injection" section: - LoadLibraryExW APC, RW path only, no shellcode - credit OpenEDR approach / sources - note VM deploy / target allowlist / StateAddPid still apply
In ApcInjection.c header: short design note + reference URLs/paths to OpenEDR files.

task.title: VM verification matrix
task.description:
Deploy matching sys + DLLs + GUI to C:\Peregrine\ on test VM only.
Cases: 1. x64 game.exe as injection target → apc_inject success, DLL hello over pipe 2. ObCallback / protect works without manual add_pid (StateAddPid still on success) 3. VAD scan on injected game: no private RX shellcode region from Peregrine 4. External cheat (cheat.exe) still detected via hooks/ETW/ObCallback as before 5. If x86 target exists: WoW64 path + PeregrineDLL_x86.dll 6. clear targets (IOCTL 14) → no inject on next launch 7. Wrong/missing DLL path → clean failure event, no BSOD 8. Process exit → no leak of pending free / protected PID list grows unbounded
Only after VM pass: mark feature tasks done.

task.title: Optional follow-ups (not required for migration done)
task.description: - Port OpenEDR-style per-process image map (ntdll/kernel32/user32) more fully - Authenticode check of inject DLL before queue (OpenEDR enableDllVerification) - Second injector backend (IAT) — only if needed later - First-thread / delayed inject retry if kernel32 path races - Multi-DLL list like OpenEDR (Peregrine currently one path per arch)

rules.rule: Copying OpenEDR injection mechanics is allowed; strip unused madCHook / IAT paths unless we explicitly want them.
Do not leave dual inject paths half-wired (shellcode + LoadLibraryExW) in production path — one primary path.
Full absolute DLL path under C:\Peregrine must keep working; re-verify LoadLibraryExW flags on VM.
Never load experimental .sys on the DEV machine that holds the repo; verify on the dedicated Windows VM.
No hacks: if LoadLibraryExW flags break non-System32 paths, fix flags/path design properly.
Mark done only after VM verification (task 8).

build.item: VM: sc stop, replace sys, sc start, elevated GUI, inject target = game.exe
